delay_description: Moving abroad or leaving residence — must physically move out before property can go live

trigger_phrases.phrase: returning to US at end of next week

typical_timeline.notes: Based on 5 timeline signals observed: extended offer to Aug 2026, unknown, unknown, unknown, before end of first week of June
observed_timelines: before end of first week of June

how_to_handle_in_meeting.acknowledge_phrase: That makes complete sense — you need to be out before guests can come in.
how_to_handle_in_meeting.timeline_question: When are you planning to move out, and is that date fixed?
how_to_handle_in_meeting.reframe_approach: Departure date = go-live opportunity. 'If you move out on [date], we can have your listing live within 3 weeks of that — potentially capturing [upcoming season].'
how_to_handle_in_meeting.how_to_keep_engaged: If departure is within 60 days: set offer expiry to match or slightly precede departure. 'You need to decide before you leave — let's use [departure date minus 2 weeks] as your deadline.'
how_to_handle_in_meeting.what_to_write_to_monday: Write departure/move date in date_mm2vmj1q. Note: 'ABROAD lead — departure date is the deadline.' Offer expiry should match departure date where possible.

conversion_after_delay.drop_off_reason: Insufficient data to determine — fewer than 10 delay entries per type
conversion_after_delay.note: First run — conversion-after-delay rates will improve with more data over time

do_not_attempt_offer_because: Unless departure is within 60 days. If departure is > 60 days away, the offer will lose urgency. Wait until lead is within 8 weeks of departure before making a time-limited offer.
